metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1275
metadata.comment:
Rationale: GABA B1 subunit detection in Globus pallidus synapses.

8/9/2017 Pim
- Literal "Most synapse-associated GABAB1 immunogold particles (87%) were observed at perisynaptic sites on the presynaptic (17%) or postsynaptic (70%) membrane (Figs. 2A,B, 4A). In contrast, at symmetric synapses, most (90%) synapse-associated GABAB1 immunogold particles were localized in the main body of the synapse (Fig. 2C-E), accounting for 34% on presynaptic and 56% on postsynaptic membrane (Fig. 4A).". Experimental description: p.341: Sprague-Dawley rats (Charles River, Margate, Kent; 200 -250 g) used in the present study were maintained on a 12 hour light/dark cycle with free access to food and water. Environmental conditions for housing of the rats, and all procedures performed on them, were in accordance with the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 (UK) and in accordance with the European Communities Council Directive (80/609/EEC). All efforts were made to minimize the suffering of the animals. -- Highlighted 11 Aug 2017
